caseStudy.title: Code Review Automation
caseStudy.description: Embedding AI review into the engineering workflow at scale.
caseStudy.overview: A large engineering organisation was experiencing code review bottlenecks, with pull requests waiting an average of 3 days for a human reviewer. Junior engineers received inconsistent feedback depending on which senior engineer reviewed their work. The goal was to augment human review with AI-powered analysis.
caseStudy.problem: Senior engineers spent 20–30% of their time on code review, creating a bottleneck for the entire organisation. Code quality was inconsistent across teams, and security vulnerabilities were occasionally merged due to reviewer fatigue. New engineers received varying levels of mentorship through review depending on team culture.
caseStudy.solution: An AI code review system was integrated into the pull request workflow, automatically analysing changes for correctness, security issues, performance implications, and style consistency. The system posted structured comments with explanations and suggested fixes. Human reviewers were presented with an AI summary to accelerate their own review.

stats.label: Avg. first feedback time (down from 3 days)

results.summary: Engineering throughput has increased substantially without reducing code quality. Junior engineers receive consistent, high-quality feedback on every pull request, accelerating their development and improving team-wide code standards.
